babyLearning.en: fully grown and snug, getting ready to meet you
babyLearning.hi: poori tarah bada aur aaram se, aapse milne ki taiyaari mein

title.hi: Tumse Milne Ka Samay Lagbhag Aa Gaya

insight.en: After all these months of waiting, the meeting you have dreamed of is finally close. Soon the little one you have carried will be in your arms.
insight.hi: in saare maheenon ke intezaar ke baad, woh mulaqaat jiske sapne aap dekhti rahi hain aakhir kareeb hai. Jald hi woh nanha jise aapne thaama hai aapki baahon mein hoga.

expanded.en: There is a tenderness in these final days that is hard to put into words. You have known your baby only through flutters and kicks, through the gentle weight you carry everywhere. Soon you will know their face, the warmth of their skin, the sound of their cry. It is natural to feel both impatient and a little afraid; both are simply love getting ready. Let yourself feel how close you are. The long becoming is nearly done, and the meeting you have waited for is almost here.
expanded.hi: in aakhri dinon mein ek narmi hai jise shabdon mein kehna mushkil hai. Aapne apne baby ko sirf halki harkaton aur laaton se jaana hai, us naram bojh se jo aap har jagah uthaati hain. Jald hi aap unka chehra jaanengi, unki tvacha ki garmjoshi, unke rone ki aawaz. Bekarari aur thoda dar dono mehsoos karna swaabhaavik hai; dono bas pyaar ki taiyaari hain. Khud ko mehsoos karne dein ki aap kitni kareeb hain. Lamba banna lagbhag poora ho gaya hai, aur woh mulaqaat jiska aapne intezaar kiya lagbhag yahaan hai.

deepDive.en: In the last weeks of pregnancy, a baby's senses are already alert — they can hear your voice clearly and will recognise it within hours of being born. The meeting is new for you both, yet your baby already knows you.
deepDive.hi: pregnancy ke aakhri hafton mein, ek baby ki indriyaan pehle se hi sajag hoti hain — woh aapki aawaz saaf sun sakte hain aur janm ke kuch ghanton mein hi use pehchaan lenge. Mulaqaat aap dono ke liye nayi hai, phir bhi aapka baby aapko pehle se jaanta hai.

remember.en: The meeting you have waited for is almost here.
remember.hi: Woh mulaqaat jiska aapne intezaar kiya lagbhag yahaan hai.

title.hi: Woh Parinde Jo Taiyaar Hue

summary.en: Two birds quietly prepare their nest, learning that readiness is made of small, loving acts.
summary.hi: Do parinde chupchaap apna ghonsla taiyaar karte hain, seekhte hain ki taiyaari chhote, pyaar bhare kaamon se banti hai.

body.en:
High in a tall tree, two birds knew their little ones would soon arrive. There was no rush in them, only a quiet busyness. Each morning they brought back something soft — a strand of wool, a feather, a wisp of dry grass — and tucked it gently into the curve of their nest.

'Is it ready yet?' the smaller bird asked one evening. 'Almost,' said the other, smoothing the lining with her beak. 'Ready is not one big thing. It is a hundred small ones, done with love.'

Day by day the nest grew rounder, warmer, softer, until it felt less like a place and more like an embrace waiting to happen. And on a still, golden morning, when the first tiny eggs lay safe inside it, the birds rested wing to wing and knew the truth at last: all their small, patient care had become a welcome. The little ones had a home to come home to.
body.hi:
ek oonche ped par ooper, do parinde jaante the ki unke nanhe jald aane wale hain. unmein koi jaldi nahi thi, sirf ek shaant vyastata. har subah woh kuch naram leke aate — oon ka ek dhaaga, ek pankh, sookhi ghaas ka ek tukda — aur use pyaar se ghonsle ki gol mein tika dete.

'kya yeh ab taiyaar hai?' chhote parinde ne ek shaam poocha. 'lagbhag,' doosre ne kaha, apni chonch se astar ko sahlaate hue. 'taiyaar hona koi ek badi cheez nahi. yeh sau chhoti cheezein hain, pyaar se ki gayi.'

din-ba-din ghonsla aur gol, aur garm, aur naram hota gaya, jab tak woh ek jagah kam aur ek aane wale aaling jaisa zyada lagne laga. aur ek shaant, sunhri subah, jab pehle nanhe ande uske andar surakshit pade the, parinde pankh se pankh laga kar aaraam karte aur aakhir sachai jaan gaye: unki saari chhoti, sabra bhari dekhbhaal ek swagat ban chuki thi. nanhon ke paas lautne ke liye ek ghar tha.

title.en: Tell your baby about the little space you have made just for them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko us chhoti jagah ke baare mein bataayein jo aapne sirf unke liye banaayi hai.

motivation.en: Describing the corner that waits for them is your first way of saying 'you belong here.'
motivation.hi: Us kone ko bataana jo unka intezaar kar raha hai, aapka 'tum yahaan ke ho' kehne ka pehla tareeka hai.

title.hi: Lagbhag Meri Baahon Mein

description.en: A short meditation imagining your first meeting
description.hi: Apni pehli mulaqaat ki kalpana karta ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: Very soon, you will rest in my arms, and we will know each other at last.
affirmation.hi: Bahut jald, tum meri baahon mein aaram karoge, aur hum aakhir ek doosre ko jaanenge.

introduction.en: Sit or lie back comfortably and let your eyes close. Take three slow breaths and rest a hand on your belly. Gently picture the moment your baby is first laid against your chest — their warmth, their weight, the soft sound of their breath. Let that quiet, happy image fill you.
introduction.hi: aaram se baithein ya letein aur aankhein band hone dein. teen dheemi saansein lein aur ek haath pet par rakhein. pyaar se us pal ko sochein jab aapka baby pehli baar aapke seene se lagaaya jaata hai — unki garmjoshi, unka bhaar, unki saans ki naram aawaz. us shaant, khush chhavi ko apne andar bharne dein.

closingThought.en: That moment is coming very soon. Carry its warmth with you through these last days.
closingThought.hi: woh pal bahut jald aa raha hai. in aakhri dinon mein uski garmjoshi apne saath le jaayein.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Us Din Ki Taraf Saans

content.en: Breathe in slowly and think 'almost here.' Breathe out and let your body soften. Just a few of these can settle the flutter of these last, waiting days.
content.hi: dheere se saans lein aur sochein 'lagbhag yahaan.' saans chhodein aur apne sharir ko naram hone dein. aise sirf kuch hi saansein in aakhri, intezaar ke dinon ki ghabraahat ko shaant kar sakti hain.

remember.en: You do not have to hurry the day; you only have to breathe towards it.
remember.hi: Aapko us din ko jaldi laane ki zaroorat nahi; bas uski taraf saans leni hai.

babyLearning.en: soft and warm, almost ready to arrive
babyLearning.hi: naram aur garm, lagbhag aane ke liye taiyaar

title.en: Ready Is A Feeling That Grows
title.hi: Taiyaar Hona Ek Ehsaas Hai Jo Badhta Hai

insight.en: You will not wake up one morning feeling perfectly ready. Readiness is not a switch — it is something that grows in you a little at a time.
insight.hi: aap kisi subah poori tarah taiyaar mehsoos karte hue nahi uthengi. taiyaari koi switch nahi hai — yeh kuch aisa hai jo aapmein thoda-thoda karke badhta hai.

expanded.en: Many mothers wait for a moment when they will finally feel 'ready,' as if a calm certainty will arrive and settle everything. But readiness rarely comes like that. It grows quietly, in the gathering of small things, in the imagining of a face, in the love that has been deepening all these months. Even if part of you still feels unsure, look closer — you are far more ready than you think. The readiness you have been waiting for has been forming inside you all along, gently, day by day.
expanded.hi: kayi maayein us pal ka intezaar karti hain jab woh aakhir 'taiyaar' mehsoos karengi, maano ek shaant nishchitta aakar sab kuch tay kar degi. par taiyaari shaayad hi aise aati hai. yeh chupchaap badhti hai, chhoti cheezon ke jamaa hone mein, ek chehre ki kalpana mein, us pyaar mein jo in saare maheenon gehra hota raha hai. agar aapka koi hissa abhi bhi anishchit lagta hai, gaur se dekhein — aap apni soch se kahin zyada taiyaar hain. jis taiyaari ka aap intezaar kar rahi thi woh hamesha aapke andar banti rahi hai, narmi se, din-ba-din.

deepDive.en: Researchers note that the sense of preparedness for parenthood comes less from knowledge and more from emotional connection — the bond you have already built is itself a form of readiness.
deepDive.hi: researchers note karte hain ki parenthood ke liye taiyaar hone ka ehsaas gyaan se kam aur bhaavnaatmak judaav se zyada aata hai — jo bond aap pehle se bana chuki hain wahi apne aap mein ek tarah ki taiyaari hai.

remember.en: You are far more ready than you feel.
remember.hi: Aap apne ehsaas se kahin zyada taiyaar hain.

title.en: The Ants Who Made Ready For The Rains
title.hi: Woh Cheentiyaan Jo Baarish Ke Liye Taiyaar Hui

summary.en: A colony of ants prepares patiently for the rains, showing that steady readiness brings calm when the big day comes.
summary.hi: Cheentiyon ka ek jhund sabra se baarish ke liye taiyaar hota hai, dikhaata hai ki sthir taiyaari bade din ke aane par shaanti laati hai.

body.en:
In a Panchatantra tale, a colony of ants saw the clouds gathering and knew the rains would soon come. They did not panic, and they did not wait. Each day, a little at a time, they carried grain into their cool earthen home and lined their tunnels snug against the wet days ahead.

A grasshopper nearby laughed at their fuss. 'Why work so hard? The skies are still blue!' But the ants only smiled and carried on, one careful step after another.

Then the rains broke, heavy and long. While the grasshopper shivered with nothing stored, the ants sat warm and well inside, listening to the water drum on the earth above. 'We are ready,' the eldest ant said softly, 'because we made ready while there was still time.' Their patient, daily care had turned a fearful season into a cosy, restful one.
body.hi:
ek Panchatantra kahani mein, cheentiyon ke ek jhund ne baadlon ko jamaa hote dekha aur jaan gaye ki baarish jald aayegi. unhone ghabraaya nahi, aur intezaar bhi nahi kiya. har din, thoda-thoda karke, woh anaaj apne thande mitti ke ghar mein le jaati aur apni surangon ko aane wale geele dinon ke khilaaf aaram se sajaati.

paas ek tiddi unki haay-tauba par hansi. 'itni mehnat kyun? aasmaan abhi bhi neela hai!' par cheentiyaan bas muskuraayi aur chalti rahi, ek sambhli huyi kadam ke baad doosri.

phir baarish toot padi, bhaari aur lambi. jab tiddi bina kuch jamaa kiye kaanp rahi thi, cheentiyaan andar garm aur theek baithi thi, ooper mitti par paani ki thaap sunti hui. 'hum taiyaar hain,' sabse buzurg cheenti ne dheere se kaha, 'kyunki humne tab taiyaari ki jab abhi samay tha.' unki sabra bhari, rozana dekhbhaal ne ek dar bhare mausam ko ek aaramdayak, sukoon bhare mausam mein badal diya tha.

title.en: Tell your baby about the little things you have gathered and saved for them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko un chhoti cheezon ke baare mein bataayein jo aapne unke liye jamaa aur sambhaal kar rakhi hain.

motivation.en: Each thing you have set aside is a small promise that they are wanted and waited for.
motivation.hi: Aapne jo bhi cheez alag rakhi hai woh ek chhota vaada hai ki unhe chaaha gaya aur unka intezaar kiya gaya.

title.hi: Raag Puriya Dhanashri

description.en: A sunset raga for a tender, settling evening
description.hi: Ek naram, thamti shaam ke liye sooryaast ka raag

affirmation.en: As the day softens into dusk, so does my heart, ready and warm for you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise din naram hokar shaam mein dhalta hai, waise hi mera dil bhi, tumhare liye taiyaar aur garm.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.hi: Thake Dinon Ke Liye Naram Oorja

content.en: If these last days leave you drained and your appetite small, reach for easy, warm and nourishing bites — a bowl of khichdi, a handful of dates and nuts, or warm milk with a little turmeric. Small, frequent meals are kinder to a crowded belly than large ones, and they keep your energy steady for the day ahead.
content.hi: agar yeh aakhri din aapko thaka dete hain aur aapki bhookh chhoti rehti hai, to aasaan, garm aur poshan dene wale niwaale lein — ek katori khichdi, mutthi bhar khajoor aur mewa, ya thodi haldi ke saath garm doodh. chhote, baar-baar ke bhojan ek bhare pet ke liye bade bhojan se zyada narm hote hain, aur woh aane wale din ke liye aapki oorja sthir rakhte hain.

remember.en: Small warm meals are easier to carry than big ones now.
remember.hi: Chhote garm bhojan ab bade bhojan se uthaane mein aasaan hain.

babyLearning.en: curled close, waiting for the right moment
babyLearning.hi: paas simta hua, sahi pal ka intezaar karta

title.hi: Ghonsla Lagbhag Taiyaar Hai

insight.en: The urge to tidy, to arrange, to make things just so is not restlessness — it is love preparing a soft place to land.
insight.hi: saaf karne, sajaane, cheezon ko theek waise banane ki ichha bechaini nahi hai — yeh pyaar ek naram jagah taiyaar kar raha hai utarne ke liye.

expanded.en: In these last days you may feel a quiet pull to ready your home — to wash tiny clothes, fold a blanket, smooth a corner that will soon hold your baby. This 'nesting' is gentle and instinctive, an old and tender wisdom in the body. It is not about a perfect room or a finished checklist. It is your heart, through your hands, making a place that says 'you are welcome here.' Let yourself enjoy it, but do not let it tire you. A nest does not need to be perfect — it only needs to be made with love.
expanded.hi: in aakhri dinon mein aapko apne ghar ko taiyaar karne ka ek shaant kheenchaav mehsoos ho sakta hai — nanhe kapde dhona, ek kambal modna, ek kona theek karna jo jald aapke baby ko thaamega. yeh 'nesting' naram aur sahaj hai, sharir mein ek purani aur naram samajh. yeh kisi perfect kamre ya poori hui checklist ke baare mein nahi hai. yeh aapka dil hai, aapke haathon ke zariye, ek aisi jagah banaate hue jo kehti hai 'tumhara yahaan swagat hai.' khud ko ise enjoy karne dein, par ise apne aap ko thakaane na dein. ek ghonsle ko perfect hone ki zaroorat nahi — bas pyaar se banaye jaane ki.

deepDive.en: The 'nesting instinct' many mothers feel near term is widely observed and likely rooted in a deep drive to prepare a safe space. Experts gently suggest channelling it without overdoing — your rest matters more than a spotless room.
deepDive.hi: 'nesting instinct' jo kayi maayein samay ke kareeb mehsoos karti hain, vyaapak roop se dekha gaya hai aur shaayad ek surakshit jagah taiyaar karne ki gehri prerna mein bhasa hai. experts pyaar se salah dete hain ki ise zyada kiye bina istemaal karein — aapka aaram ek bedaag kamre se zyada maayne rakhta hai.

remember.en: A nest only needs to be made with love, not made perfect.
remember.hi: Ek ghonsle ko bas pyaar se banaye jaane ki zaroorat hai, perfect nahi.

title.hi: Woh Raat Jab Krishna Aaye

summary.en: On a dark and stormy night, the world quietly readies itself to welcome the beloved child Krishna.
summary.hi: Ek andheri toofani raat ko, duniya chupchaap pyaare bachche Krishna ka swagat karne ke liye taiyaar hoti hai.

body.en:
Long ago, on a night of heavy rain, a baby was about to be born in the city of Mathura. The skies were dark and the river Yamuna ran high, yet the whole world seemed to hush in tender waiting. In a quiet cell, the mother Devaki held her breath, and her heart was full of both fear and longing.

Then, at the deepest hour of night, the little one Krishna arrived. They say the prison locks fell open on their own, and the guards drifted into gentle sleep. The mighty Yamuna, rushing and wild, grew calm to let the baby pass safely to a loving home in Gokul.

It is told that all of nature made ready that night — the rain, the river, the stars — as if the whole world were leaning in to welcome one small, beloved child. Some arrivals, the story says, the entire universe prepares for.
body.hi:
bahut samay pehle, bhaari baarish ki ek raat ko, Mathura nagri mein ek baby janm lene wala tha. aasmaan andhera tha aur Yamuna nadi oonchi beh rahi thi, phir bhi poori duniya naram intezaar mein chup si lagti thi. ek shaant kothri mein, maa Devaki ne apni saans roki, aur unka dil dar aur tadap dono se bhara tha.

phir, raat ke sabse gehre pehar mein, nanha Krishna aaya. kehte hain ki jail ke taale apne aap khul gaye, aur pehredaar naram neend mein chale gaye. vishaal Yamuna, behti aur jangli, shaant ho gayi taaki baby surakshit Gokul ke ek pyaar bhare ghar tak pahunch sake.

kaha jaata hai ki us raat saari prakriti taiyaar hui — baarish, nadi, taare — maano poori duniya ek chhote, pyaare bachche ka swagat karne jhuk rahi ho. kuch aagman aise hote hain, kahani kehti hai, jinke liye poora brahmaand taiyaari karta hai.

title.en: Tell your baby who is getting ready and waiting to meet them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki kaun unse milne ke liye taiyaar ho raha aur intezaar kar raha hai.

motivation.en: Naming the people who long to hold them tells your baby they are arriving into open, waiting arms.
motivation.hi: Un logon ko naam dena jo unhe thaamne ko taras rahe hain, aapke baby ko batata hai ki woh khuli, intezaar karti baahon mein aa rahe hain.

title.en: I Am Ready To Welcome You
title.hi: Main Tumhara Swagat Karne Ke Liye Taiyaar Hoon

affirmation.en: My heart, my arms and our home are open and ready. Whenever you choose to come, you will be welcomed with love.
affirmation.hi: Mera dil, meri baahein aur hamara ghar khule aur taiyaar hain. Tum jab bhi aana chuno, tumhara pyaar se swagat hoga.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.hi: Mera Ghar Kaafi Taiyaar Hai

content.en: My baby does not need a perfect room or a finished list. They need my arms and my love, and those are already here.
content.hi: Mere baby ko ek perfect kamra ya poori hui list nahi chahiye. Unhe meri baahein aur mera pyaar chahiye, aur woh pehle se yahaan hain.

remember.en: Your love is the only nursery your baby truly needs.
remember.hi: Aapka pyaar hi ekmaatra nursery hai jiski aapke baby ko sach mein zaroorat hai.

babyLearning.en: settled and snug, treasuring these last quiet days
babyLearning.hi: basa aur aaram se, in aakhri shaant dinon ko sanjota

title.en: The First Days Will Be A Blur Of Love
title.hi: Pehle Din Pyaar Ki Ek Dhundh Honge

insight.en: The first days with your newborn will pass in a soft, tired haze. Do not try to do them perfectly — just let yourself be soaked in love.
insight.hi: apne navjaat ke saath pehle din ek naram, thaki dhundh mein guzrenge. unhe perfect tareeke se karne ki koshish na karein — bas khud ko pyaar mein bheeg jaane dein.

expanded.en: When your baby finally arrives, the days will lose their usual shape. There will be little sleep, much feeding, and long, quiet hours of simply gazing at a face you cannot stop looking at. It can feel overwhelming and tender all at once. Do not measure those first days by tidiness or productivity. They are not meant to be managed — they are meant to be felt. Let the world wait. Let the dishes pile. The only thing those early days ask of you is to be present, and to let yourself fall completely in love.
expanded.hi: jab aapka baby aakhir aata hai, dinon ka apna aam aakaar kho jaata hai. thodi neend hogi, bahut feeding, aur lambe, shaant ghante bas ek chehre ko nihaarte hue jise dekhna aap rok nahi paatin. yeh ek saath bhaari aur naram lag sakta hai. un pehle dinon ko saaf-suthrepan ya productivity se mat naapein. unhe sambhaalna nahi hai — unhe mehsoos karna hai. duniya ko intezaar karne dein. bartan jamaa hone dein. un shuruaati dinon ko aapse bas itna chahiye ki aap maujood rahein, aur khud ko poori tarah pyaar mein girne dein.

deepDive.en: The early postpartum weeks are sometimes called the 'fourth trimester' — a time when both baby and parent are still adjusting. Experts say rest and skin-to-skin closeness matter far more than chores in these days.
deepDive.hi: shuruaati postpartum hafton ko kabhi-kabhi 'chautha trimester' kaha jaata hai — ek samay jab baby aur parent dono abhi bhi adjust ho rahe hote hain. experts kehte hain ki in dinon mein aaram aur tvacha-se-tvacha kareebi gharelu kaamon se kahin zyada maayne rakhti hai.

remember.en: Those first days are to be felt, not managed.
remember.hi: Woh pehle din mehsoos karne ke liye hain, sambhaalne ke liye nahi.

title.hi: Taiyaar Kiya Gaya Ghonsla

summary.en: In a Jataka tale, a wise bird readies a safe nest, teaching that loving preparation protects what we hold most dear.
summary.hi: Ek Jataka kahani mein, ek samajhdaar pakshi ek surakshit ghonsla taiyaar karta hai, sikhaata hai ki pyaar bhari taiyaari un cheezon ko bachaati hai jo humein sabse pyaari hain.

body.en:
In a Jataka tale, a wise mother bird lived in a great tree at the edge of the forest. Before her little ones were due, she did not simply build anywhere. She studied the branches, choosing one that was strong, sheltered from the harshest winds and hidden from prying eyes.

Day after day she wove her nest with care, lining it deep with the softest down so that nothing sharp could ever touch her young. The other birds, in their haste, built quick and careless nests on exposed limbs.

When the storms of the season came, many of those hasty nests were shaken loose. But the wise bird's little ones lay safe and warm, rocked gently in their well-made home. 'I could not stop the storms,' she said softly, 'but I could make ready a place where my little ones would be held through them.' Loving preparation, the tale teaches, is its own kind of protection.
body.hi:
ek Jataka kahani mein, ek samajhdaar maa pakshi jungle ke kinare ek bade ped par rehti thi. apne nanhon ke aane se pehle, usne bas kahin bhi nahi banaya. usne tehniyon ko parkha, ek aisi chuni jo mazboot thi, sabse kathor hawaon se bachi aur taakte aankhon se chhupi.

din-ba-din usne apna ghonsla dhyan se buna, use sabse naram roon se gehra astar diya taaki koi nukeeli cheez kabhi uske nanhon ko chhoo na sake. doosre pakshi, apni jaldi mein, khule angon par tez aur laaparwaah ghonsle banaate.

jab mausam ke toofan aaye, un jaldbaaz ghonslon mein se kayi hil kar gir gaye. par samajhdaar pakshi ke nanhe surakshit aur garm pade rahe, apne achhe bane ghar mein pyaar se jhoolte. 'main toofanon ko rok nahi sakti thi,' usne dheere se kaha, 'par main ek aisi jagah taiyaar kar sakti thi jahaan mere nanhe unke beech thaame jaate.' pyaar bhari taiyaari, kahani sikhaati hai, apne aap mein ek tarah ki suraksha hai.

title.en: Tell your baby about the very first cuddle you are dreaming of giving them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko us sabse pehle gale lagaane ke baare mein bataayein jo aap unhe dene ka sapna dekhti hain.

motivation.en: Picturing that first hold aloud lets your baby feel the warmth that is waiting for them.
motivation.hi: Us pehle aaling ko bolkar sochna aapke baby ko us garmjoshi ko mehsoos karaata hai jo unka intezaar kar rahi hai.

title.hi: Do Ke Liye Ek Shaant Jagah

description.en: A short meditation to rest gently with your baby
description.hi: Apne baby ke saath narmi se aaram karne ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: In this quiet moment, there is nothing to do and nowhere to be — only you and me, resting close.
affirmation.hi: Is shaant pal mein, kuch karna nahi aur kahin hona nahi — sirf tum aur main, paas aaram karte.

introduction.en: Settle into a comfortable position and let your shoulders drop. Take a few slow, deep breaths and feel your body grow soft and heavy. Imagine a warm, quiet room where only you and your baby rest together, with all the time in the world and nothing that needs doing.
introduction.hi: ek aaramdayak position mein baithein aur apne kandhon ko girne dein. kuch dheemi, gehri saansein lein aur apne sharir ko naram aur bhaari hote mehsoos karein. ek garm, shaant kamra sochein jahaan sirf aap aur aapka baby saath aaram karte hain, duniya bhar ka samay liye aur kuch bhi karne ki zaroorat ke bina.

closingThought.en: Carry this stillness with you — soon you will share many quiet moments like this, awake and together.
closingThought.hi: is sthirta ko apne saath le jaayein — jald aap aise kayi shaant pal baatenge, jaagte aur saath.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.hi: Naram Pet Ki Saans

content.en: Rest both hands low on your belly. Breathe in slowly and feel them rise; breathe out and feel them fall. Let five gentle breaths slow everything down around you.
content.hi: dono haath apne pet ke neeche rakhein. dheere se saans lein aur unhe uthte mehsoos karein; saans chhodein aur unhe girte mehsoos karein. paanch naram saansein aapke around sab kuch dheema kar dein.

remember.en: Slowing your breath is the simplest way to slow a racing mind.
remember.hi: Apni saans dheemi karna ek daudte mann ko dheema karne ka sabse saral tareeka hai.

babyLearning.en: tucked in close, growing more ready each day
babyLearning.hi: paas dubka hua, har din aur taiyaar hota

title.hi: Halka Saaman, Bhaara Pyaar

insight.en: Your baby will not remember which things you had ready. They will only ever feel how much they were loved.
insight.hi: aapka baby yaad nahi rakhega ki aapke paas kaun si cheezein taiyaar thi. woh hamesha sirf yeh mehsoos karega ki use kitna pyaar kiya gaya.

expanded.en: In these last days it is easy to worry about getting everything right — the perfect bag, the right list, the missing item. But babies arrive needing remarkably little: warmth, milk, closeness, and a steady heartbeat to rest against. The truest preparation was never about objects at all. It was the love you have been growing all these months, ready to pour out the moment you meet. Pack what you need without fuss, and carry the heavier, more precious thing lightly in your heart — you already have more than enough to give.
expanded.hi: in aakhri dinon mein sab kuch theek karne ki chinta karna aasaan hai — perfect bag, sahi list, gum hui cheez. par babies behad kam zaroorat ke saath aate hain: garmjoshi, doodh, kareebi, aur ek sthir dhadkan jiske saath aaram karein. sabse sachi taiyaari kabhi cheezon ke baare mein thi hi nahi. yeh woh pyaar tha jo aap in saare maheenon badhaati rahi hain, milte hi undelne ke liye taiyaar. jo zaroorat ho woh bina haay-tauba ke pack karein, aur us bhaari, zyada keemti cheez ko apne dil mein halke se le jaayein — aapke paas pehle se dene ke liye kaafi se zyada hai.

deepDive.en: Newborns are soothed most by the simplest things — a parent's smell, warmth, voice and heartbeat. Studies show skin-to-skin contact alone steadies a baby's breathing and temperature better than most equipment.
deepDive.hi: navjaat sabse saral cheezon se sabse zyada shaant hote hain — ek parent ki gandh, garmjoshi, aawaz aur dhadkan. studies dikhaati hain ki akeli tvacha-se-tvacha kareebi ek baby ki saans aur taapmaan ko zyaadatar saamaan se behtar sthir karti hai.

remember.en: Your baby needs your love far more than any list of things.
remember.hi: Aapke baby ko cheezon ki kisi bhi list se kahin zyada aapke pyaar ki zaroorat hai.

title.hi: Birbal Aur Sammaanit Mehmaan

summary.en: Clever Birbal shows that a true welcome lies in warmth of heart, not in show.
summary.hi: Chalaak Birbal dikhaata hai ki sacha swagat dil ki garmjoshi mein hai, dikhaawe mein nahi.

body.en:
One day Emperor Akbar asked his courtiers, 'When an honoured guest arrives, what makes them feel most welcome — a grand feast or a fine palace?' The ministers spoke of golden plates, silken cushions and lavish halls. But Birbal stayed quiet.

'And you, Birbal?' the emperor asked. Birbal bowed. 'Your Majesty, may I show you tomorrow instead of telling you?'

The next evening Birbal welcomed Akbar not to a palace but to his own simple home. There was no gold, only a clean mat, warm food cooked with care, and a family whose eyes shone with genuine gladness. Akbar felt more at ease than in any grand hall.

'You see, Your Majesty,' Birbal smiled, 'a guest is not welcomed by the size of the house, but by the warmth of the hearts inside it. A true welcome is felt, not displayed.'
body.hi:
ek din Badshah Akbar ne apne darbaariyon se poocha, 'jab ek sammaanit mehmaan aata hai, use sabse zyada swagat kaisa mehsoos karaata hai — ek shaandaar daawat ya ek sundar mahal?' mantriyon ne sone ki thaaliyon, reshmi gaddiyon aur aalishaan kamron ki baat ki. par Birbal chup raha.

'aur tum, Birbal?' Badshah ne poocha. Birbal jhuka. 'jahaanpanah, kya main aapko batane ke bajaaye kal dikha doon?'

agli shaam Birbal ne Akbar ka swagat kisi mahal mein nahi balki apne saade ghar mein kiya. wahaan sona nahi tha, sirf ek saaf chatai, pyaar se pakaaya garm khaana, aur ek parivar jiski aankhein sachi khushi se chamak rahi thi. Akbar ko kisi bhi shaandaar kamre se zyada sukoon mehsoos hua.

'dekha, jahaanpanah,' Birbal muskuraaya, 'ek mehmaan ka swagat ghar ke aakaar se nahi, balki uske andar ke dilon ki garmjoshi se hota hai. ek sacha swagat mehsoos kiya jaata hai, dikhaaya nahi jaata.'

title.en: Tell your baby how you are resting and saving your strength for them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap unke liye kaise aaram kar rahi hain aur apni taakat bacha rahi hain.

motivation.en: Letting your baby hear that you are caring for yourself shows them that love includes rest, too.
motivation.hi: Apne baby ko yeh sunaana ki aap apna khayal rakh rahi hain unhe dikhaata hai ki pyaar mein aaram bhi shaamil hai.

title.en: I Rest, And That Is Enough
title.hi: Main Aaram Karti Hoon, Aur Yeh Kaafi Hai

affirmation.en: I do not need to do more to be ready. Resting now is how I gather my strength for you.
affirmation.hi: Taiyaar hone ke liye mujhe aur karne ki zaroorat nahi. Abhi aaram karna hi woh tareeka hai jisse main tumhare liye apni taakat jamaa karti hoon.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar affirmations are gentle, spiritually-rooted sentences a mother shares with her baby. The calm and love behind your words begin shaping a peaceful inner world for you both, long before your baby is born.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar affirmations woh pyaari, aadhyatmik vaakya hain jo ek maa apne baby se kehti hai. Aapke shabdon ke peeche ki shaanti aur pyaar, baby ke janm se bahut pehle hi, aap dono ke liye ek shaant andaruni duniya banane lagte hain.

howToUse.en: Sit quietly, rest a hand on your belly, and read the affirmation slowly — once silently, once aloud. Let yourself believe it for these few moments. A minute is enough, and repeating it at the same time each day makes it a calming ritual.
howToUse.hi: Shaanti se baithein, ek haath pet par rakhein, aur affirmation dheere se padhein — ek baar mann mein, ek baar bolkar. In kuch palon ke liye use sach maan lein. Ek minute kaafi hai, aur ise har din ek hi samay dohraana ise ek shaant ritual bana deta hai.

title.en: I Am Allowed To Slow Down
title.hi: Mujhe Dheema Hone Ki Ijaazat Hai

content.en: These last days are not for finishing everything. They are for gathering myself, gently, before the great change ahead.
content.hi: Yeh aakhri din sab kuch khatam karne ke liye nahi hain. Yeh khud ko jamaa karne ke liye hain, narmi se, aage aane wale bade badlaav se pehle.

remember.en: Slowing down now is preparation, not laziness.
remember.hi: Abhi dheema hona taiyaari hai, aalas nahi.

babyLearning.en: resting deeply, saving energy for the big day
babyLearning.hi: gehraai se aaram karta, bade din ke liye oorja bachaata

title.hi: Aage Ke Liye Abhi Aaram Karein

insight.en: The rest you take in these last days is not idle time. It is you quietly storing strength for the journey just ahead.
insight.hi: in aakhri dinon mein aap jo aaram leti hain woh bekaar samay nahi hai. yeh aap chupchaap aage ki yatra ke liye taakat jamaa kar rahi hain.

expanded.en: When the finish line feels so close, there can be a pull to stay busy, to use every hour. But the days ahead will ask a great deal of you — long nights, deep effort, a body and heart stretched in new ways. The kindest, wisest thing you can do now is rest. Nap when you can. Let others carry what they can carry. Sit and simply be. This is not falling behind; it is filling the well you will soon draw from. Rest now, gently and without guilt — your future self, and your baby, will thank you.
expanded.hi: jab manzil itni kareeb lagti hai, vyast rehne, har ghante ka istemaal karne ka kheenchaav ho sakta hai. par aane wale din aapse bahut kuch maangenge — lambi raatein, gehri mehnat, ek sharir aur dil jo naye tareekon se khinche jaate hain. abhi aap jo sabse dayaalu, sabse samajhdaar cheez kar sakti hain woh hai aaram. jab ho sake jhapki lein. doosron ko jo woh utha sakte hain uthaane dein. baithein aur bas rahein. yeh peeche rehna nahi hai; yeh us kuye ko bharna hai jisse aap jald paani lengi. abhi aaram karein, narmi se aur bina guilt ke — aapka aane wala roop, aur aapka baby, aapka shukriya karega.

deepDive.en: Studies suggest that mothers who enter labour well-rested tend to cope with it more steadily. Rest in late pregnancy is not indulgence — it is genuine preparation for the work ahead.
deepDive.hi: studies sujhaati hain ki jo maayein achhi tarah aaram kiye labour mein jaati hain woh use zyada sthirta se sambhaalti hain. der se pregnancy mein aaram aish nahi hai — yeh aage ke kaam ke liye sachi taiyaari hai.

remember.en: Resting now is filling the well you will soon draw from.
remember.hi: Abhi aaram karna us kuye ko bharna hai jisse aap jald paani lengi.

title.hi: Pyaar Se Bana Jhoola

summary.en: A village grandfather carves a cradle slowly and lovingly, showing that what is made with care holds love within it.
summary.hi: Ek gaon ka dadaji ek jhoola dheere aur pyaar se gadhta hai, dikhaata hai ki jo dhyan se banaya jaata hai usmein pyaar bhasa hota hai.

body.en:
In an old folk tale, a grandfather learned that a new baby would soon come to his family. He was a simple man with rough, weathered hands, and he had little money to give. So he went into the forest, found a fallen branch of soft wood, and began to carve.

Night after night, by the light of a small lamp, he shaped a little cradle. He worked slowly, smoothing every edge so nothing could ever scratch the baby's skin, humming old lullabies as he carved. The villagers told him to hurry, but he only smiled. 'A cradle made in haste rocks a baby,' he said. 'A cradle made with love holds one.'

When the baby finally arrived and was laid in that gentle cradle, it slept more soundly than anywhere else. For woven into every smooth curve of the wood was something no money could buy — the patient love of one who had made ready.
body.hi:
ek purani lok kahani mein, ek dadaji ko pata chala ki ek naya baby jald unke parivar mein aane wala hai. woh ek saada aadmi tha jiske haath kharadre aur thake the, aur uske paas dene ko kam paisa tha. to woh jungle mein gaya, naram lakdi ki ek giri huyi shaakh dhoondhi, aur gadhna shuru kiya.

raat-dar-raat, ek chhote deepak ki roshni mein, usne ek nanha jhoola gadha. usne dheere kaam kiya, har kinaare ko aisa sahlaaya ki kuch bhi baby ki tvacha ko kabhi khuraad na sake, gadhte hue purani loriyaan gungunaata. gaon waalon ne use jaldi karne ko kaha, par woh bas muskuraaya. 'jaldi mein bana jhoola ek baby ko jhulaata hai,' usne kaha. 'pyaar se bana jhoola ek ko thaamta hai.'

jab baby aakhir aaya aur us naram jhoole mein litaaya gaya, woh kahin aur se zyada gehri neend soya. kyunki lakdi ke har naram mod mein kuch buna tha jise koi paisa nahi khareed sakta — ek aise ka sabra bhara pyaar jisne taiyaari ki thi.

title.en: Tell your baby about the warm welcome that is waiting for them.
title.hi: Apne baby ko us garm swagat ke baare mein bataayein jo unka intezaar kar raha hai.

motivation.en: Describing the welcome ahead lets your baby arrive already knowing they are deeply wanted.
motivation.hi: Aage ke swagat ko bataana aapke baby ko pehle se yeh jaante hue aane deta hai ki unhe gehraai se chaaha gaya hai.

title.hi: Raag Sindhura

description.en: An evening raga for tenderness and quiet love
description.hi: Narmi aur shaant pyaar ke liye ek shaam ka raag

affirmation.en: As this tender melody settles around me, so does a deep, restful love for you.
affirmation.hi: Jaise yeh naram dhun mere around thamti hai, waise hi tumhare liye ek gehra, aaramdayak pyaar bhi.

about.en: Ragas are classical Indian melodies, each tied to a mood and time of day. From about week 18 your baby can hear; even now, the calm a raga brings you surrounds your baby and gently shapes a peaceful environment around your pregnancy.
about.hi: Raag classical Bharatiya dhunein hain, har ek kisi mood aur din ke samay se judi. Lagbhag 18ve hafte se aapka baby sun sakta hai; abhi bhi, raag se aane wali shaanti aapke baby ko gher leti hai aur aapki pregnancy ke around ek shaant mahaul banati hai.

howToUse.en: Play the raga softly while you sit or rest with a hand on your belly. You needn't focus hard — let the sound fill the room as you breathe slowly. Two to five minutes, ideally at the raga's time of day, makes it a ritual your baby will one day recognise.
howToUse.hi: Raag dheere se chalaayein jab aap baithein ya pet par haath rakh kar aaram karein. Zyada dhyan lagane ki zaroorat nahi — aawaz ko kamre mein bhar jaane dein jab aap dheere saans lein. Do se paanch minute, ho sake to raag ke samay par, ise ek aisa ritual bana deta hai jise aapka baby ek din pehchaanega.

title.hi: Aakhri Hafte Ke Bhaaripan Ko Halka Karna

content.en: If a full belly feels heavy and uncomfortable now, lean on light, hydrating foods — soft fruits, curd, soups and plenty of water. Sip warm drinks slowly and eat a little at a time. Keeping gently hydrated also eases the swelling and tiredness that often come in these final days.
content.hi: agar ab ek bhara pet bhaari aur asahaj lagta hai, to halke, paani-bhare khaane par bharosa karein — naram phal, dahi, soup aur khoob paani. garm peyon ki dheere chuski lein aur thoda-thoda khaayein. narmi se hydrated rehna us soojan aur thakaan ko bhi halka karta hai jo aksar in aakhri dinon mein aati hai.

remember.en: Light, hydrating foods sit easiest in these final, heavy days.
remember.hi: Halke, paani-bhare khaane in aakhri, bhaari dinon mein sabse aasaani se baithte hain.

babyLearning.en: fully ready, simply waiting for the perfect moment
babyLearning.hi: poori tarah taiyaar, bas sahi pal ka intezaar karta

title.hi: Aapki Baahein Lagbhag Bhar Gayi Hain

insight.en: Soon the arms that have rested on your belly all these months will hold your baby for real. The waiting is nearly over.
insight.hi: jald woh baahein jo in saare maheenon aapke pet par tiki rahi hain aapke baby ko sach mein thaamengi. intezaar lagbhag khatam hai.

expanded.en: For nine long months your arms have wrapped around a belly, holding your baby in the only way you could. Soon those same arms will cradle a real, breathing little one — soft and warm and finally here. It is almost impossible to imagine, and yet it is so close now. Whatever these final days hold, let yourself feel the wonder of how near you are. The long wait, the wondering, the hoping — all of it has been leading to this. Very soon, the arms that have held you in waiting will simply hold your child.
expanded.hi: nau lambe maheenon se aapki baahein ek pet ke ird-gird liptii rahi hain, aapke baby ko us ekmaatra tareeke se thaame jo aap kar sakti thi. jald wahi baahein ek asli, saans lete nanhe ko jhulaayengi — naram aur garm aur aakhir yahaan. ise sochna lagbhag namumkin hai, phir bhi yeh ab itna kareeb hai. yeh aakhri din jo bhi laayein, khud ko us hairaani ko mehsoos karne dein ki aap kitni kareeb hain. lamba intezaar, sochna, ummeed karna — yeh sab isi taraf le ja raha tha. bahut jald, woh baahein jisne aapko intezaar mein thaama, bas aapke bachche ko thaamengi.

deepDive.en: From the very first minutes after birth, holding your baby close releases hormones in you both that calm the heart and deepen the bond. Those waiting arms are part of how your baby first learns the world is safe.
deepDive.hi: janm ke pehle minute se hi, apne baby ko paas thaamna aap dono mein aise hormones chhodta hai jo dil ko shaant karte hain aur bond ko gehra karte hain. woh intezaar karti baahein us tareeke ka hissa hain jisse aapka baby pehli baar seekhta hai ki duniya surakshit hai.

remember.en: Very soon, your waiting arms will simply hold your child.
remember.hi: Bahut jald, aapki intezaar karti baahein bas aapke bachche ko thaamengi.

title.hi: Bhor Se Theek Pehle

summary.en: A nature parable in which the whole world holds its breath in the last quiet hour before sunrise, teaching that the deepest waiting comes right before the joy.
summary.hi: Ek prakriti drishtaant jisme poori duniya sooryoday se pehle aakhri shaant ghante mein apni saans roke rehti hai, sikhaata hai ki sabse gehra intezaar khushi se theek pehle aata hai.

body.en:
There is an hour just before dawn when the whole world seems to hold its breath. The night birds have grown quiet, the stars have softened, and the sky is neither dark nor light but a deep, waiting grey. It is the stillest hour of all.

In this hush, the flowers wait with their petals closed, the trees stand silent, and the river runs low and slow. Everything seems to know that something is coming. The waiting feels long, and sometimes it feels as though the light may never break at all.

And then, softly, at the edge of the sky, the first thread of gold appears. The birds begin to sing, the petals begin to open, and the whole world wakes into warmth and colour. The deepest waiting, the old parable says, always comes right before the dawn — and the dawn, when it comes, is worth every quiet hour of the wait.
body.hi:
ek ghanta hota hai bhor se theek pehle jab poori duniya apni saans roke si lagti hai. raat ke pakshi chup ho gaye hain, taare naram pad gaye hain, aur aasmaan na andhera hai na ujla balki ek gehra, intezaar karta sleti. yeh sabse sthir ghanta hota hai.

is chuppi mein, phool apni pankhudiyaan band kiye intezaar karte hain, ped khaamosh khade hain, aur nadi neeche aur dheere behti hai. sab kuch jaanta sa lagta hai ki kuch aane wala hai. intezaar lamba lagta hai, aur kabhi-kabhi aisa lagta hai ki shaayad roshni kabhi phootegi hi nahi.

aur phir, narmi se, aasmaan ke kinaare, sone ka pehla dhaaga prakat hota hai. pakshi gaana shuru karte hain, pankhudiyaan khulne lagti hain, aur poori duniya garmjoshi aur rang mein jaag uthti hai. sabse gehra intezaar, purana drishtaant kehta hai, hamesha bhor se theek pehle aata hai — aur bhor, jab aati hai, intezaar ke har shaant ghante ke laayak hoti hai.

title.en: Tell your baby that you are counting the days now, gently and full of love.
title.hi: Apne baby ko bataayein ki aap ab din gin rahi hain, narmi se aur pyaar se bhari.

motivation.en: Letting your baby hear your tender impatience tells them how longed-for their arrival truly is.
motivation.hi: Apne baby ko apni naram bekarari sunaana unhe batata hai ki unka aana kitna tarsa hua hai.

title.hi: Saath Ke Aakhri Shaant Din

description.en: A short meditation to savour your final days as one
description.hi: Ek hone ke apne aakhri dinon ko sanjone ka ek chhota dhyan

affirmation.en: These quiet days carrying you are precious. Soon we meet, but for now, I hold you close in the only way I know.
affirmation.hi: Tumhe uthaane ke ye shaant din keemti hain. Jald hum milenge, par abhi ke liye, main tumhe us ekmaatra tareeke se paas thaamti hoon jo main jaanti hoon.

introduction.en: Sit comfortably and rest both hands on your belly. Take three slow breaths and feel your baby close. Let yourself savour this — the gentle weight, the small movements, the quiet closeness of these final days when you and your baby are still one. Breathe slowly and simply be here, together.
introduction.hi: aaram se baithein aur dono haath apne pet par rakhein. teen dheemi saansein lein aur apne baby ko paas mehsoos karein. khud ko ise sanjone dein — woh naram bhaar, woh chhoti harkatein, in aakhri dinon ki shaant kareebi jab aap aur aapka baby abhi bhi ek hain. dheere saans lein aur bas yahaan rahein, saath.

closingThought.en: Soon you will hold this little one in your arms — but these last quiet days together are a gift of their own.
closingThought.hi: jald aap is nanhe ko apni baahon mein thaamengi — par saath ke ye aakhri shaant din apne aap mein ek tohfa hain.

about.en: Garbh Sanskar meditations are short, gentle practices to connect with your baby — not generic mindfulness, but a few minutes of sending warmth and love inward. Your calm becomes your baby's calm.
about.hi: Garbh Sanskar meditations chhote, pyaare abhyaas hain apne baby se judne ke liye — koi aam mindfulness nahi, balki kuch minute garmjoshi aur pyaar andar bhejne ke. Aapki shaanti aapke baby ki shaanti ban jaati hai.

howToUse.en: Find a quiet spot, sit comfortably and close your eyes. Follow the introduction, breathe slowly, and rest your attention gently on your baby. Two to three minutes is plenty. Doing it daily, ideally at the same hour, deepens the bond.
howToUse.hi: Ek shaant jagah chunein, aaram se baithein aur aankhein band karein. Introduction follow karein, dheere se saans lein, aur apna dhyan pyaar se apne baby par tikaayein. Do se teen minute kaafi hai. Ise rozana, ho sake to ek hi samay karne se bond gehra hota hai.

title.en: A Breath For The Days To Come
title.hi: Aane Wale Dinon Ke Liye Ek Saans

content.en: Breathe in slowly and gather a little calm. Breathe out and let go of any worry about when and how. Repeat softly five times, trusting that your body and your baby know the way.
content.hi: dheere se saans lein aur thodi shaanti jamaa karein. saans chhodein aur kab aur kaise ki har chinta ko jaane dein. dheere se paanch baar dohraayein, yeh bharosa karte hue ki aapka sharir aur aapka baby raasta jaante hain.

remember.en: Your body and your baby already know the way.
remember.hi: Aapka sharir aur aapka baby pehle se raasta jaante hain.
